overview: This Black/Green Modern deck leverages fast, evasive creatures and graveyard recursion to apply immediate pressure. Street Wraith provides early board presence and life total manipulation, while Archon of Cruelty serves as a hard removal spell that doubles as a formidable attacker. Stitcher's Supplier ensures your best threats return from the graveyard, creating a relentless cycle of value that overwhelms opponents before they can stabilize.
win_conditions: Direct combat damage from Archon of Cruelty and recurring creatures
Life drain and life gain triggers from Street Wraith deaths
Graveyard recursion loops via Stitcher's Supplier to outpace opponent's resources

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two one- or two-mana creatures
Hands containing Street Wraith or Archon of Cruelty
Hands with early land drops to support the curve
Keep 4+ lands, at least one early threat (Street Wraith) or interaction (Fatal Push, Dismember, Go for the Throat).
Keep Stitcher's Supplier only if paired with a land or early play.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with only three-mana or higher cards
Hands with zero lands or four+ lands
Mulligan hands with 3+ lands and no early plays, or multiple removal spells with no threats/lands.

early_game: Play Street Wraith on turn one to establish flying pressure and begin life manipulation
Prioritize land drops to ensure Archon of Cruelty can be cast by turn two
Use early creatures to force opponent's removal spells
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Street Wraith or Birds of Paradise if available.
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast Street Wraith if not played, or hold interaction for opponent's threats.
Sequence fetches/shocks to ensure black mana on turn 1-2 for Fatal Push, then green for threats.
prioritize board control and graveyard setup.
mid_game: Cast Archon of Cruelty to remove key threats while establishing a 4/4 body
Trigger Stitcher's Supplier to replay Street Wraith or Archon of Cruelty from the graveyard
Maintain board presence to force opponent into unfavorable trades
do not burn removal on expendable tokens.
Manage your graveyard carefully—Stitcher's Supplier and Archon of Cruelty both require it, but avoid filling it with unplayable cards.
Use discard to disrupt opponent's hand while preserving your own resources.
Commit threats only when you have removal for their answer or when you can trade favorably.
Hold back Archon of Cruelty until you have a clear path to cast it or can use it as a sac outlet.
late_game: Cycle Archon of Cruelty repeatedly to generate value and combat damage
Leverage Stitcher's Supplier to continuously refill the board with recurring threats
Grind down opponent's life total through attrition and life drain triggers
Archon of Cruelty is the primary win condition.
Get it to the graveyard via discard, sacrifice, or death triggers, then cast it.
Follow up with Stitcher's Supplier recursion or additional threats to close out the game.
Go all-in when you have answered their key threats, have a clear path to cast Archon, and can protect it or use it as a sac outlet.
Commit when you can trade favorably and have interaction left for their response.

key_cards.name: Archon of Cruelty
key_cards.notes: Cast to exile opponent's key threats, then use the 4/4 body to close out games. Be mindful of the self-damage trigger when it leaves the battlefield.

key_cards.name: Stitcher's Supplier
key_cards.notes: Sacrifice or let it die to return Street Wraith or Archon of Cruelty to the battlefield, creating infinite value loops and board presence.

key_cards.name: Street Wraith
key_cards.notes: Play on turn one to apply flying pressure. Its death trigger drains opponent life while stabilizing your own, making it ideal for baiting removal.

sequencing_tips: Hold Archon of Cruelty until you can cast it for maximum impact, such as removing a game-winning threat
Sacrifice Stitcher's Supplier immediately after it resolves or when it dies naturally to avoid wasting its trigger
Play Street Wraith before casting Archon of Cruelty to maximize early pressure and life swing
Manage your life total carefully, as Archon of Cruelty's departure deals damage to you
Archon of Cruelty is a sorcery, so cast it when you can protect it or use it as a sac outlet.
protect it or use it as a sacrifice target.
Watch for graveyard hate and adjust your game plan accordingly.
Do not overextend with Street Wraith
it is a tool, not a win condition.

matchups.advice: Play aggressively early to force removal spells before they stabilize. Use Archon of Cruelty to clear their key counters or sweepers.
Prioritize life gain with Street Wraith to extend the game. Use Archon of Cruelty to remove their biggest threats and buy time for Stitcher's Supplier recursion.
Race them by applying constant pressure. Recycle threats with Stitcher's Supplier to outvalue their resource advantage.

Use discard to remove counters, cast Archon at end step or when they lack mana, or use it as a sacrifice outlet for Stitcher's. Avoid casting it into known counters; instead, pressure with early threats and removal.
Apply early pressure with Street Wraith and hold interaction for their mana dorks or finishers. Do not let them get ahead; use Fatal Push and Dismember to disrupt their acceleration.

Win the value war by using discard to disrupt their graveyard and hand. Trade efficiently, prioritize Stitcher's Supplier recursion, and use Blood Moon if available. Out-value them by managing your graveyard better and closing with Archon when they run out of answers.

common_threats: Flying creatures that bypass your ground blockers
Board wipes that clear your recurring threats
Direct damage spells that bypass your life gain strategy
Wide creature spreads that overwhelm your removal
Watch for graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Leyline of the Void, Surgical Extraction) and discard spells.
Track opponent's hand for counters or removal.
Note when they start casting their win conditions
Pay attention to their graveyard size for Stitcher's value.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Archon of Cruelty, Stitcher's Supplier, Street Wraith

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Street Wraith, Archon of Cruelty, Stitcher's Supplier

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Archon of Cruelty, Stitcher's Supplier, Street Wraith
